mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time over any 3-year rolling window, or if the correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and forward returns is not significantly positive (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate rises above 5%, it signals an aggressively restrictive monetary policy stance that tightens financial conditions and depresses economic activity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS > 5% but the average 20-day forward SPX return is positive or not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) across all occurrences, or if the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and an increase in the unemployment rate indicates a weakening housing market feeding into broader labor weakness, a classic precursor to consumer-led recessions that produce sustained negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% month-over-month and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points in the same month
failure_condition: If the joint condition occurs but the subsequent 60-day SPX return is positive in more than 40% of cases, or if the model's binary prediction accuracy (sign) is below 55% over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of low consumer price inflation (CPI YoY below 3%), robust growth in industrial production signals genuine economic expansion without overheating, which is accretive to corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If in low inflation regimes, INDPRO growth > 0.5% is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more than 45% of the time, or if the regime definition (CPI < 3%) does not produce a higher return than the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) above 0.5% indicates robust consumer demand that bolsters corporate revenues and stock performance over a short weekly horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth above 0.5% is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or if the signal yields an average return no different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising federal funds rate and rising unemployment rate represents a stagflationary shock – monetary tightening is failing to cool an overheating labor market – which severely depresses equity valuations as both discount rates rise and earnings expectations fall.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has increased over the prior 3 months and UNRATE has increased over the prior 1 month
regime_hint: monetary tightening with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: If the joint increase condition produces positive 20-day SPX returns more than 40% of the time, or if the interaction term in a linear model is not statistically significant at 10% level over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts from its recent peak signals the onset of a housing market downturn that leads to negative wealth effects and reduced construction activity, ultimately depressing economic growth and equity returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% from its 12-month maximum
failure_condition: If a 10% decline in HOUST from the peak is not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if the average return following the trigger is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP reflect broad economic expansion that lifts corporate earnings across sectors, providing a fundamental tailwind to S&P 500 index returns over a quarterly investment horizon.
failure_condition: If sequential quarterly GDP increases are associated with negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 40% of quarters, or if the correlation between GDP growth and forward returns is not positive (Rank IC < 0.03).
